Accurate as of May 13, 2026  2:37 PM. MAY 27 2026 Office Visit/Return with Dan Vardi Wednesday May 27 2:00 PM (Arrive by 1:45 PM) Deaconess Pulmonary Critical Care 519 Harriet Street Evansville IN 47710-1715 812-450-7720 MAY 29 2026 Pain Management Consultation with Dr Esebua Friday May 29 7:00 AM (Arrive by 6:30 AM) Deaconess Comprehensive Pain Center Medical Office Building 3 4015 Gateway Blvd, Suite 2120 Newburgh IN 47630-9460 812-842-2737 Today's medication changes Return in about 6 weeks for Follow up with doc next time. Located on the first floor of the detached parking garage. Look for the big dark gray awning labeled Deaconess Critical Care. What's Next Instructions • • • • • • • You saw Caitlin Miller, ACNP on Wednesday May 13, 2026. The following issues were addressed: COPD, very severe (HCC) Dependence on non-invasive ventilation Chronic respiratory acidosis Chronic hypoxemic respiratory failure (HCC) Panlobular emphysema (HCC) Palliative care patient Hospital discharge follow-up 110/74 Blood Pressure 31.41 BMI 183 lb Weight 5' 4" Height 45 Pulse 96% Oxygen Saturation Before your next appointment, you can verify your information before you arrive. Up to one weeks prior to your appointment, you can review and make changes to your contact information, insurance, or health history information directly in MyChart, saving you time at registration. Simply click on your upcoming appointment in MyChart to get started. Once you arrive for your appointment, just check in at the front desk. MyChart Today's Visit

If needed may administer additonal one spray to opposite nostril after 2-3 minutes. omeprazole 40 MG capsule Commonly known as: PRILOSEC Take 1 capsule (40 mg) by mouth 1 (one) time each day Indications: Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease ondansetron 8 MG disintegrating tablet Commonly known as: ZOFRAN ODT Take 1 tablet (8 mg) by mouth every 8 (eight) hours if needed for Nausea predniSONE 10 MG tablet Commonly known as: DELTASONE Take 4 tablets by mouth daily x1 day, THEN 3 tabs daily x3 days, THEN 2 tabs daily x7 days, THEN 1.5 tabs daily x10 days, THEN 1 tab daily x21 days. Start taking on: May 6, 2026 sulfamethoxazole-trimethoprim 800-160 MG per tablet Commonly known as: BACTRIM DS Take 1 tablet by mouth three times a week for 75 doses THEOPHYLLINE PO Yupelri 175 MCG/3ML nebulizer solution Generic drug: revefenacin Take 3 mL (175 mcg) by nebulization daily  very important  * This list has 2 medication(s) that are the same as other medications prescribed for you. Read the directions carefully, and ask your doctor or other care provider to review them with you.

English (CSN: 852303873) • Printed at 5/13/2026  2:37 PM Page 5 of 6       Did you know that as a patient you can request an estimate for the cost of non-emergent services?  Go to Deaconess.com/pricing, e-mail DeaconessCostQuote@deaconess.com, or call 812-450-COST (2678) for more details.  All estimates will be provided within five business days.          Patients can request an estimate of the costs for nonemergency health care services from their health care providers and the health facility. For an estimate, please contact 812-450-2678(COST) or email deaconesscostquote@deaconess.com. Patient Satisfaction If you receive a patient satisfaction survey, please tell us how we are doing. If you have concerns, comments or questions, please list those on the survey or feel free to contact us at: 812-450-7720. Additional Information Price Estimates Available!

# Page 6^page=6
Lisa A. English (CSN: 852303873) • Printed at 5/13/2026  2:37 PM Page 6 of 6   • • • • • • • • • • • • • • General Information for Health Lifestyle:   Maintaining a healthy weight and active lifestyle is ideal for cardiopulmonary health. Healthy food choices can extend longevity and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, diabetes, hypertension, and cancer, among other important chronic diseases. A healthy diet can also help with weight management and improve health-related quality of life. Portion control along with choosing WHOLE FOODS more often and eating less processed foods will benefit you. When choosing whole foods, think of fruits, vegetables, whole grains, nuts, seeds, meats, and legumes. Avoid highly processed foods or foods that have a very long ingredient list at the grocery store. Physical activity is associated with decreased mortality and a greater likelihood of healthy aging (survival with intact physical and cognitive functioning) "Start low and go slow" is a good rule of thumb with the recognition that patients can start well below recommended guidelines and gradually build up. As little as 5 to 10 minutes a day of aerobic exercise with one or two simple functional strength-based exercises may be a good starting point for some patients. If you are able to, even daily walking will yield benefit.   Enjoy meals and exercise with family and friends when possible.   Some tips:   Eat your veggies and fruits — and switch to whole grains. An abundance and variety of plant foods should make up the majority of your meals. They should be minimally processed, and try to purchase them when they're in season. Strive for seven to 10 servings a day of veggies and fruits. Switch to whole-grain bread and cereal, and begin to eat more whole-gain rice and pasta products. Keep baby carrots, apples and bananas on hand for quick, satisfying snacks. Fruit salads are a wonderful way to eat a variety of healthy fruit. Go nuts. Keep almonds, cashews, pistachios and walnuts on hand for a quick snack. Choose natural peanut butter, rather than the kind with hydrogenated fat added. Spice it up. Herbs and spices make food tasty and are also rich in health-promoting substances. Season your meals with herbs and spices rather than salt when possible. Go fish. Eat fish once or twice a week. Fresh or water-packed tuna, salmon, trout, mackerel and herring are healthy choices. Grilled fish tastes good and requires little cleanup. Reduce high fat meats such as sausage, bacon and other high-fat meats. Choose low-fat dairy. Limit higher fat dairy products such as whole or 2 percent milk, cheese and ice cream. Switch to skim milk, fat-free yogurt and low-fat cheese.     WEIGHT GOALS:   Your current Estimated body mass index is 31.9 kg/m² as calculated from the following: Height as of 4/25/26: 5' 4" (1.626 m). Weight as of 5/5/26: 185 lb 13.6 oz (84.3 kg). (underweight is less than 19; ideal=19-25; overweight=25-30; obese is above 30). Instructions
